update: Mom seems to be more active than normal, making small adjustments to the nest from time to time.
funfact: ROBIN'S DON'T MATE FOR LIFE: When it comes to romance, robins like to keep things casual. Like many migratory birds, they do not form long-term pair bonds and often choose new mates each spring. Males usually arrive first to claim territory. Once the females show up, the males put on a show by singing, strutting, shaking their wings, fluffing their tail feathers, and even offering food. After a seasonal pair forms, they mate between April and July and often raise two to three broods in a single season.
